name: Lobster and Roasted Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 cup corn, cut off the cob
2 medium baking potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
2 stalks green onions, fine bias cut
Salt and ground black pepper - to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam lobster for 17 minutes, let cool, then remove from shell. Save shells to make lobster stock if desired.
2. Saute bacon until crispy in a sauce or small stock pot.
3. Add onions; green, red, yellow, and jalapeno peppers; celery and carrots, cook until soft.
4. Add tomato paste and green chilies. If using a lobster base over stock, add it now. Cook for 3 minutes stirring constantly over medium heat.
5. Add half of the unsalted butter and cook until melted. Add flour and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
6. Roast corn kernels in an oven on a baking sheet or flat pan until slightly browned and add to chowder.
7. Add cream style corn, southwestern seasoning, and smoked hamhocks. If using stock, add it now. Cook for 1/2 hour, keeping at a slow boil and stirring constantly.
8. Add potatoes and cook for another 15 minutes. If too thick, add more stock or water to desired consistency.
9. Add green onions, cilantro, and lemon juice. Slowly whisk in cream and the remaining butter until melted.
10. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Serves 12 people.
